Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember the goal is open ended questions that cover missing details decision criteria roles responsibilities timing sequencing exception handling right No SQL or implementation stuff just conceptual understanding of the onboarding process they described

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets start with the initial documentation step Wait the interviewee said the coordinator requests updates if docs are missing outdated but what are the specific rules for whats considered up to date Like how old can an inspection report be before its invalid Also are there different documentation requirements for different property types like student housing vs luxury vs furnished Oh right and who is responsible for covering the cost of getting updated docs the landlord or the company Thats a missing detail

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next the property manager assignment step They listed factors workload geographic specialization experience with property type track record with unique landlord needs But how are those factors weighted Like if a manager is at 90 percent capacity but is the only one in that area with luxury unit experience do they still get assigned Also is there a formal approval step for the assignment or is it just the coordinators call What if a landlord has a pre existing relationship with a specific manager does that override the standard criteria

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the introductory call between manager and landlord They mentioned discussing special conditions like pets maintenance responsibilities payment channels But are there any pre set company wide policies that cant be changed by the landlord even if requested Like are there mandatory clauses in all leases for things like entry notice maintenance response times Also who documents the outcomes of that call and where is that information stored so all teams compliance marketing leasing have access

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next the inspection step If the inspector finds issues the manager works with the landlord to address them But whats the threshold for what issues can be fixed vs what would make the property ineligible for onboarding Like if a property has a foundational issue that would cost six figures to fix do they reject it or work out a plan with the landlord Also who approves the repair budgets and whats the typical timeline between inspection and required repairs before the property can move forward

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then marketing step The digital team creates listings based on inspection results and pricing models How are rental price ranges determined especially if the landlord has a expected price thats higher than market rate Do they have a process for negotiating that with the landlord or is there a maximum variance allowed between landlords expected price and the companys market based pricing Also who approves the final marketing materials the property manager the landlord or both

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then tenant screening step They mentioned credit checks employment verification previous landlord references But what are the minimum eligibility criteria that are non negotiable across all properties Are there different criteria for different property types or locations like student housing might have different income requirements Also when multiple tenants are interested whats the formal priority order Like do longer leases always take precedence or are there other factors like credit score income stability What about if a landlord has a preference for a specific tenant like a family vs a group of students does that override the companys standard screening criteria as long as its fair housing compliant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the quality checks step compliance officer reviews lease financial analyst checks pricing IT checks system integration But what happens if one of these checks fails Like if the compliance officer finds a lease clause that violates fair housing who is responsible for revising it and whats the process for getting landlord sign off on the revised clause Also how long does the entire quality check phase typically take and are there SLAs for resolving issues that come up during that phase

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Now exception handling The interviewee mentioned exceptions like special insurance custom lease clauses But whats the formal process for flagging and approving exceptions Like if a landlord wants a custom clause thats not on the standard lease who has the authority to approve it the property manager a regional director a legal team Also are there certain types of exceptions that are categorically not allowed regardless of landlord request And how are exceptions tracked across the process so that all teams are aware of them when interacting with the property or landlord

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also communication flow They said communication is tracked via email CRM project management tools But are there formal communication checkpoints scheduled at each phase of onboarding or is communication ad hoc Whats the escalation path if theres a delay at any step like if the landlord doesnt provide updated docs within a certain timeframe Also how is information shared with external stakeholders like landlords and tenants throughout the process do they have access to a portal or do they only get updates via email call from the property manager

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Timing and sequencing The interviewee walked through the steps but what are the typical timelines for each phase Like how long does documentation verification usually take how long from assignment to the introductory call how long after inspection to marketing launch Also are there any steps that can happen in parallel or do all steps have to be completed sequentially For example can the marketing team start preparing materials while the inspection is ongoing or do they wait for the inspection report

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Roles and responsibilities Wait the interviewee mentioned regional offices coordinators property managers leasing agents compliance officers financial analysts IT marketing But are there specific handoff points between each role Like once the coordinator finishes documentation verification who do they formally hand the property off to and what information do they need to provide Also if theres an issue that spans multiple teams like a repair thats going to push back the move in date affecting marketing and leasing who is responsible for coordinating communication between those teams

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait also post onboarding No wait the process is onboarding but wait are there any steps that loop back Like if the tenant screening takes longer than expected do they pause marketing or keep the listing active Also what happens if a property fails the final quality checks after the lease is signed Like if IT finds a system error do they have to delay the tenants move in

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Oh and also landlord communication expectations Does the company have a standard SLA for responding to landlord inquiries during onboarding And how are progress updates proactively shared with the landlord rather than them having to follow up

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Wait also for properties with unique requirements like student housing or furnished rentals are there modified steps in the onboarding process For example do furnished rentals require a separate inventory inspection or additional documentation from the landlord about the furnitures condition

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Also whats the threshold for when a property is considered fully onboarded Is it when the first tenant moves in or when all quality checks are passed or when the lease is signed

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait lets organize these into clear open ended questions grouped by theme maybe Lets make sure theyre not leading open ended cover all the areas missing details decision criteria roles responsibilities timing sequencing exception handling communication

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets check if these are all open ended no SQL no implementation just conceptual Lets make sure they dont ask for technical stuff

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait maybe also a question about loopbacks Like if during tenant screening it becomes clear that the propertys amenities dont match what was marketed whats the process for updating marketing materials or addressing the discrepancy with prospective tenants Oh maybe add that Or is that covered Wait maybe also how do you handle situations where a landlord changes their requirements mid onboarding like they decide they no longer want to allow pets after the marketing materials have already been published with pet friendly messaging

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Wait lets make that a question How do you handle mid onboarding changes to property details or landlord requirements without creating confusion for prospective tenants or compliance gaps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Also maybe a question about performance tracking Like What key metrics do you track to measure the efficiency and effectiveness of the onboarding process and how do you use those metrics to identify bottlenecks or areas for improvement Wait thats good because its about understanding how they evaluate the process which is part of deepening understanding

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets make sure we dont have any closed ended questions All of these are open ended Lets organize them logically following the flow of the process so its natural for the interviewee

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait lets reorder them to follow the onboarding flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait thats 13 questions but theyre all targeted open ended cover all the required areas missing details decision criteria roles and responsibilities timing and sequencing exception handling None of them ask for SQL or implementation details right All conceptual

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Perfect That should cover all the gaps in the original process description and help deepen understanding of how the process actually works in practice not just the high level steps the interviewee mentioned
